figure.title: Virtual Pods (vPods) Architecture
figure.purpose: Demonstrates the concept and flexibility of Virtual Pods (vPods) within a physical UALink Pod.

technical_summary: Highlights how physical Accelerators across different System Nodes can be logically grouped into vPods.
Virtual Pod 1: Contains 2 Accelerators from System Node 1.
Virtual Pod 2: Spans across nodes, containing 2 Accelerators from System Node 1 and 3 Accelerators from System Node 2.
Virtual Pod 3: Contains all 4 Accelerators from System Node 3.
The UALink Pod Controller manages these vPod assignments and isolations over the fabric.

components.name: Virtual Pod (vPod)
components.description: A logically isolated subset of Accelerators grouped for a specific workload or tenant.

critical_facts: vPods provide logical slicing of the physical infrastructure, allowing arbitrary groupings of Accelerators across different physical chassis/nodes into unified compute clusters.
